effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical repo-wide record sweep + renders + tolerance removal; breadth not depth; runs last in the release.

design_decisions.choice: EARS classification: map each retired AC 'type' to the canonical 'ears_pattern' and repoint the ears.go reader (fold sworn#95 into S12)
options: (a) accept the sworn lint EARS-classification degradation to all-Ubiquitous across the five migrated releases as a tracked precision loss
(b) fold the type->ears_pattern reshape plus the ears.go/classifySpecJSON reader repoint into S12 so classification is preserved (atomic coupled code+data change)
(c) sequence a separate follow-on slice after S12 to migrate the reader
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach) ratified 2026-07-11 (captain-proceed.md, pin 1 disposition): option (b) — FOLD INTO S12. The migration MAPS the retired 'type' to the canonical 'ears_pattern' (ubiquitous->ubiquitous, event-driven->event-driven, state-driven->state-driven, optional-feature->optional-feature, complex->complex, unwanted->unwanted-behaviour, note->note), drops 'ears_keyword', and repoints ears.go/classifySpecJSON to read 'ears_pattern'. Homed in S12 (not re-opening verified S11) because the code change must land atomically with the record reshape. Spec amended on release-wt @4c01dff (AC-07 added). Tracked sworn#95.
design_decisions.rationale: Stripping 'ears_keyword' for strict v0.10.0 spec-v1 (which allows only id/text/ears_pattern/test_refs) without repointing the reader would silently collapse every non-ubiquitous AC across five releases to Ubiquitous in `sworn lint` (classifySpecJSON reads the now-absent ears_keyword and defaults empty->Ubiquitous). Preserving classification requires the coupled reader migration, so it is architecturally significant (a field-deleting migration + reader contract change across five releases).

design_decisions.choice: board.json migration by whitelist projection to {$schema, release, tracks:{id,slices,depends_on}} rather than the shim's field blacklist
design_decisions.rationale: A whitelist (vs the shim's blacklist) also drops the stray top-level 'activity' key and the release/track worktree+state fields that strict board-v1 (additionalProperties:false) forbids, and is future-proof against any other derived stray field. Reversible and local to the board reshape; matches S11 Pin-1 pure-plan. Captain acknowledged (review pin 4).

design_decisions.choice: AC-03/AC-06 validation sweep implemented as a records-conformance Go test globbing the five spec-v1-era releases and asserting baton.ValidateSchema per spec.json/board.json
design_decisions.rationale: No existing CLI surface runs baton.ValidateSchema over on-disk records (write paths use the lenient baton.Validate; sworn doctor checks render-drift/timestamps). A Go test is the HOW for the spec-mandated strict draft-2020-12 sweep, doubling as durable CI regression (a future un-migrated record fails CI) and Rule 1 reachability (real records through the real strict validator). One test file beyond declared touchpoints; Captain acknowledged (review pin 3).
